---
title: ""
---

## Youwei Hu

### Graduate Researcher | Biostatistics & Data Science
<div class="row">
<div class="col-sm-4">

![](images/selfy.png){width=100%}

</div>

<div class="col-sm-8">
I'm a graduate student in Biostatistics at Emory University with a background in financial mathematics. My work focuses on applying statistical modeling, machine learning, and data visualization to solve complex problems in healthcare and public health. 

I have experience in EEG-based biomarker analysis for Alzheimer's disease and Bayesian modeling of infectious disease dynamics, with a strong interest in translating data into actionable insights.

</div>
</div>

---

**Research Interests:**

- Bayesian hierarchical modeling and statistical inference  
- Neuroimaging and EEG-based biomarkers for neurological disorders  
- Infectious disease modeling and viral shedding dynamics  
- Integration of heterogeneous biomedical data (e.g., Ct values and viral load)  
- Data visualization and interactive tools for health data analysis  

[My Projects](projects.html) | [CV](files/CV.pdf) | [GitHub](https://github.com/hww228)
